title: PlanRecord
description: The optimizer service's envelope around one OptimizerPlan for one cycle - it stamps on the provenance and outcome the model must not be trusted to invent (run id, chosen horizon window, backend identity, gate decision). Layer 2 of the plan contract; returned by GET .../plans/latest and referenced by escalations. See docs/specs/design/optimizer/05-spec-optimizer-plan-contract.md §3.

ReasonCode.description: Canonical escalation reason code - the single source of truth is the optimizer interfaces spec (Escalation reason codes). Present on outcome only when status is escalated.

Backend.description: Which model and prompt produced the plan. A fallback is a different model held to its own evaluation baseline, so failover is recorded here, not hidden; prompt_version records the exact prompt template so a stored plan is traceable to its (model, prompt) provenance.

model.description: The pinned model id (e.g. llama3, claude-sonnet-4-6).

prompt_version.description: The pinned planner prompt-template version that produced this plan (e.g. v1), resolving climate-optimizer/prompts/planner.v{version}.md. Co-located with model because (model, prompt_version, sampling) is the provenance tuple a plan is reproduced from; a change is a deliberate ADR event that re-captures the per-backend evaluation baselines.

role.description: Whether the primary backend or the configured fallback produced this plan.

Horizon.description: The adaptive window the service chose for the cycle: 12h by default, 24h near a day boundary. trajectory spans [start, end].

start.description: Horizon start (RFC 3339, UTC) - the cycle start.

Outcome.description: What the gates decided. reason_code is required when status is escalated (enforced by the if/then below); message is the optional operator-facing detail.

status.description: applied = submitted to Phase 2; escalated = surfaced, not applied; extended = no new plan applied - the state-change gate skipped the LLM (or there was nothing to refine) and the prior plan or Phase 2 baseline is carried forward.

schema_version.description: Major version of this plan contract (internal; a bump is an ADR event).

optimizer_run_id.description: The cycle's trace / provenance id. Phase 2 records it on the applied bundle; every plan and escalation is traced by it (P3-OBS-1).

greenhouse_id.description: The greenhouse this plan is for.

created_at.description: When the cycle produced the plan (RFC 3339, UTC).
